summary: Iteration 50: All 3 critical bug fixes verified. Backend: 7/7 tests passed. Frontend: All UI components verified.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py lines 56-76: Device binding correctly checks restaurant security_settings.device_binding_enabled before enforcing. Only blocks when enabled=true AND device mismatch.
auth.py line 60: device_binding_enabled defaults to False when not set
auth.py line 57: Admin/platform_owner roles are excluded from device binding checks
restaurants.py line 169: SecuritySettingsUpdate model includes device_binding_enabled field with default False
restaurants.py lines 172-186: GET /restaurants/my/security returns device_binding_enabled field
restaurants.py lines 189-205: PUT /restaurants/my/security updates device_binding_enabled in security_settings
RestaurantSettings.js lines 809-823: Device Binding toggle with data-testid='device-binding-toggle' in Anti-Time Theft card
RestaurantSettings.js line 649: Staff row uses 'flex flex-col sm:flex-row' for responsive layout
push.js lines 39-47: try/catch around checkPermissions and requestPermissions with (non-fatal) message
push.js lines 55-60: try/catch around register() with (non-fatal) message - prevents app crash
seed_data_creation: Device binding reset for 'user' account during testing. device_binding_enabled reset to false after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 50 verified 3 critical bug fixes: (1) Device binding is now a toggle (default OFF) in Settings > Security > Anti-Time Theft card. Login works from any device when OFF. Only blocks when admin enables it AND there's a device mismatch. (2) Staff row in User Management uses flex-col on mobile (430px), flex-row on desktop - names and actions visible on all screen sizes. (3) Push notification service has try/catch around checkPermissions, requestPermissions, and register calls to prevent app crash on native devices. All backend tests pass. All frontend UI verified.

device_binding_toggle_can_enable.evidence: PUT /api/restaurants/my/security can toggle device_binding_enabled to true/false

login_succeeds_when_binding_disabled.evidence: POST /api/auth/login with device_id succeeds when device_binding_enabled=false (multiple devices work)

login_blocks_when_binding_enabled_and_mi.evidence: POST /api/auth/login returns 403 with 'device_blocked' when device_binding_enabled=true and device mismatch

admin_not_affected_by_device_binding.evidence: Admin logins work with any device_id regardless of device_binding_enabled setting

login_without_device_id_always_works.evidence: Login without device_id (web browser) works even when device_binding_enabled=true

device_binding_toggle_ui.evidence: Settings > Security > Anti-Time Theft card shows Device Binding toggle with data-testid='device-binding-toggle'

staff_names_visible_mobile.evidence: User Management at 430px width shows staff names clearly visible with flex-col layout

staff_row_responsive_layout.evidence: Staff row uses 'flex flex-col sm:flex-row' - stacks vertically on mobile, horizontal on desktop

push_notification_try_catch.evidence: push.js has try/catch around checkPermissions (line 39-47), requestPermissions (line 42), and register (line 55-60) with (non-fatal) messages
